title: Have you read spoilers or what
display_title: Have you read spoilers or what
wikitext:
[]: Have you read spoilers or what?             Quote Post by slothen » Wed Sep 26, 2007 12:50 am Yeah, so I'm fairly certain that the majority of the community here has not traveled to Japan to see rebuild. That said, have most of you read spoilers and reviews? I, for one, have been waiting for a subbed torrent to come out, but that seems like it could be awhile in the making. I haven't read any reviews or spoilers of plot changes or details. Am I in the minority here?

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Wed Sep 26, 2007 2:21 am I would guess that you are very much in the minority. The new Angel of Doom trailer has loads of spoilers, and I've read pretty much every review I could find. I intended on waiting for a subbed version, too. But, as you can see, I didn't last very long!

[]:             Quote Post by Millions Livio » Wed Sep 26, 2007 2:55 am I've read and watched everything that has came out.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Sep 26, 2007 3:16 am Spoilers one hundred percent okay for me on Rebuild. Same for Halo 3, which releases today (I like spoilers :twisted: )

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Wed Sep 26, 2007 4:43 am Yep, i'm such a sucker for spoilers! And seeing some posts around this section, i can say that you are in the minority slothen.

[]:             Quote Post by ThroneofDravaris » Wed Sep 26, 2007 4:58 am Not really bothered by spoilers since this volume probably wasn't going to diverge from the anime a lot anyway. I might resist for the subsequent volumes if it looks like they'll be making significant changes.

[]:             Quote Post by marmotgirl » Wed Sep 26, 2007 5:10 am I came home from vacation, clicked on to A Certain Board, couldn't unsee the [ SPOILER ] DUDE, KAWORU'S ON THE MOON [ / SPOILER] in huge glaring capitals in the middle of the page, and just gave up trying to hide after that. I was going to try and hide from 2.0 spoilers, but honestly, the speculation is far too much fun. I've found with certain other series that I follow/have followed (The Tsubasa manga at the moment, and the FMA anime in the past) that trying to avoid spoilers whilst maintaining an active internet presence is more stressful than just giving in and being spoiled. :roll:

[]:             Quote Post by Sandoz » Wed Sep 26, 2007 9:09 am I've spoiled myself rotten. I'm impatient, and it's not as if I'm unfamiliar with the characters or very basic storyline, anyway.

[]: Re: Have you read spoilers or what?             Quote Post by fadingreminder » Wed Sep 26, 2007 12:57 pm slothen wrote: Am I in the minority here?

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Wed Sep 26, 2007 2:17 pm You can put me in the same category as fadingreminder and slothen when it comes to avoiding spoilers. I do so because I do not want to be disappointed by giving my hopes up. I also try to keep my expectations low.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Wed Sep 26, 2007 8:13 pm I have also been avoiding spoilers, hence my almost complete lack of posts in the Rebuild forum. What's the point of knowing everything that happens in a movie before you see it? Why even bother watching it?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Sep 26, 2007 8:32 pm Well, it looks and sounds fucking awesome. I'd be religiously avoiding spoilers as well, and I will for 2.0, unless somehow I find myself back in Japan next year.

[]:             Quote Post by Naz » Wed Sep 26, 2007 10:20 pm I haven't watched anything but the very first trailer

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Wed Sep 26, 2007 11:37 pm Attention those avoiding spoilers [spoiler] Snape kills Trinity with Rosebud!!! [/spoiler] Ok, I lied... Misato does it.

[]:             Quote Post by fadingreminder » Thu Sep 27, 2007 10:50 am cat42 wrote: Ok, I lied... Misato does it.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Sep 27, 2007 7:15 pm Seeing as I've already listened to (like, several times already ^^; ) the Rebuild 1.0 audio, I've been spoiling myself silly with reading things about it. Though I hope to keep myself Spoiler-free with 2.0 and the rest. Meh. We'll see. After all, this is coming from the Fan who read all of Evaotaku FAQ and Guide to Evangelion before she even WATCHED Eva (as well as watching some episodes, including EoE out of order) and yet was STILL puzzled at both endings. ; ("Like both, but...seriously. WTF did I just watch?? ")

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Thu Sep 27, 2007 7:19 pm I read almost all the spoilers

[]:             Quote Post by destroyer » Sat Sep 29, 2007 3:45 am i read the spoilers basically because i didn't think it would be too different until the last movie. i feel like i was really really really wrong now.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sat Sep 29, 2007 12:24 pm goddamn when will i be able to see it? driving me crazy..

[]:             Quote Post by JP » Sat Sep 29, 2007 1:00 pm Spoiler, Sachiel dies. :roll: No, but you know what I mean. There's not much that I consider "spoilers". I eagerly await anything new about the films.
summary: []: Have you read spoilers or what?             Quote Post by slothen » Wed Sep 26, 2007 12:50 am Yeah, so I'm fairly certain that the majority of the community here has not traveled to Japan to see rebuild. That said, have most of you read spoilers and reviews? I, for one, have been waiting for a subbed torrent to come out, but that seems like it could be awhile in the making. I haven't read any reviews or spoilers of plot changes
